channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: AFL tribunal examines Collard’s homophobic slur and Butters’ umpire abuse allegations
item.description: The AFL tribunal convened on April 14, 2026, to address two high-profile disciplinary cases involving St Kilda’s Lance Collard and Port Adelaide’s Zak Butters. Collard was found to have used a homopho...

item.title: Finance Minister outlines plans to manage reserves as Pakistan prepares to repay UAE loan
item.description: Pakistan’s Finance Minister Muhammad Aurangzeb stated that the government is weighing several financing options, including Eurobonds, Islamic sukuk, and commercial loans, to replace a $3.5 billion loa...

item.title: Stanford researchers identify peptide that may suppress appetite with fewer side effects than Ozempic
item.description: Researchers at Stanford Medicine have identified a naturally occurring peptide that could potentially replicate some of the appetite-suppressing and weight-loss effects of semaglutide (marketed as Oze...

item.title: Philippines Maintains Stable Investment Rating from Moody's
item.description: Moody’s, a major debt watcher, has affirmed the Philippines’ 'Baa2' stable investment rating. This means the country’s creditworthiness remains at a level considered good, and investors can still feel...

item.title: Cape Town Officers Assaulted During Athlone Arrest Amid Tensions and Heavy Rains in Joburg
item.description: Cape Town law enforcement officers once again faced violence in the city after being attacked while carrying out an arrest in Athlone. According to official reports, officers responded to gunfire in t...

item.title: East African Artists Encouraged to Collaborate for Regional Creative Industry Development
item.description: During the recent East African Art Nexus (TEANEX) conference at the University of Nairobi, Tanzanian representative Adrian Nyangamale, who serves as President of the Federation of Applied Arts, emphas...

item.title: Canberra’s Our Hours bakery brings the taste of Korea to Dickson
item.description: Set on a quiet corner in Dickson, Canberra, the Korean bakery Our Hours is reshaping local tastes with soft, pillowy breads and pastries steeped in Korean tradition. Founded by Alex Choi, his wife Ell...

item.title: Royal Opera outlines ambitious 2026–27 season amid funding pressures and recent controversies
item.description: Oliver Mears, director of opera at the Royal Opera House in Covent Garden, has outlined the company’s forthcoming 2026–27 season while reflecting on a year marked by both artistic success and institut...

item.title: Man charged after Molotov attack on Sam Altman’s home and OpenAI office
item.description: US federal prosecutors have charged a 20-year-old man, Daniel Moreno-Gama, over an alleged Molotov cocktail attack targeting the home of OpenAI CEO Sam Altman in California. According to officials, th...

item.title: Jets eliminated from playoff race after 6-2 loss to Golden Knights in Vegas
item.description: The Winnipeg Jets’ 2025–26 NHL season came to an end on Monday night after a 6-2 defeat to the Vegas Golden Knights, officially eliminating them from playoff contention. Although the elimination was c...

item.title: Washington enforces Gulf blockade as tensions with Iran deepen over oil and nuclear demands
item.description: The United States has initiated a naval blockade on Iranian ports in the Gulf region, marking a sharp escalation in the six‑week‑old conflict between the US‑Israeli coalition and Iran. The blockade, a...

item.title: LA Teacher Negotiations Stall, Threatening Historic Strike
item.description: The Los Angeles Unified School District (LAUSD) is on the brink of a historic walkout as contract negotiations between the district and the United Teachers Los Angeles (UTLA) have failed to yield a re...

item.title: Financial literacy training empowers over 2,000 rural women in Namtumbo District
item.description: Over 2,000 women from five wards in Namtumbo District have undergone financial literacy training aimed at boosting their economic independence and protecting them from fraud in informal savings and le...

item.title: Dozens detained in New York City protest against US arms sales to Israel and military support for Israel
item.description: In New York City, around 90 protesters were detained during a demonstration opposing US arms sales to Israel and calling for an end to American military support for Israel. The protest, held on Monday...

item.title: Energy CS Opiyo Wandayi distances himself from Sh12 billion fuel import deal during parliamentary probe
item.description: Energy Cabinet Secretary Opiyo Wandayi appeared before the National Assembly Energy Committee to respond to concerns surrounding a Sh12 billion fuel import arrangement that has sparked controversy wit...

item.title: Independent Commission for Infrastructure transfers luxury cars linked to graft probe to Ombudsman
item.description: The Independent Commission for Infrastructure (ICI) officially turned over two luxury vehicles to the Office of the Ombudsman as part of ongoing investigations into alleged corruption cases involving ...

item.title: A Wind in the Willows-inspired stay at a stylish Chilterns B&B in Oxfordshire and Berkshire
item.description: A travel writer explores the Chilterns in Oxfordshire and Berkshire, inspired by Kenneth Grahame’s classic The Wind in the Willows. Staying at Bonni B&B in Hill Bottom, a design-led one-bedroom apartm...

item.title: Edo Police Nab Six Officers and Three Civilians for Extortion and Abduction Scheme
item.description: The Edo State Police Command don arrest nine people wey dem say dey involved for harassment, abduction, and extortion of residents for Benin City. Among the suspects, six be police officers — five wey...

item.title: Masoud Juma Finds New Stability and Purpose with Kuta Collection FC After Lebanon Stint
item.description: Harambee Stars striker Masoud Juma has opened up about his challenging recent experiences abroad and his current efforts to regain competitive rhythm through Kuta Collection FC. The forward recently r...

item.title: Ontario government proposes major reduction in powers of school board trustees amid concerns over spending
item.description: The Ontario government is moving forward with legislation that would significantly reduce the authority of elected school board trustees while keeping the positions in place. Education Minister Paul C...
